     The Numbers Business Report

Everest (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $43,482,270Details
International Box Office $177,814,791
Worldwide Box Office $221,297,061
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $4,891,025
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $5,124,299
Total $10,015,324
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Inspired by the events surrounding an attempt to reach the summit of the world’s highest mountain, Everest documents the awe-inspiring journey of two different expeditions challenged beyond their limits by one of the fiercest snowstorms ever encountered by mankind. Their mettle tested by the harshest elements found on the planet, the climbers will face nearly impossible obstacles as a lifelong obsession becomes a breathtaking struggle for survival.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$7,222,035 (16.6% of total gross)
Legs:3.28 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:19.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$65,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:545 opening theaters/3,009 max. theaters, 3.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $58,337,421

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 18th, 2015 (Limited) by Universal
September 25th, 2015 (IMAX) by Universal
September 25th, 2015 (Expands Wide) by Universal
International Releases: August 17th, 2015 (Wide) (Slovakia)
September 10th, 2015 (Wide) (Peru)
September 16th, 2015 (Wide) (Egypt)
September 16th, 2015 (Wide) (Philippines)
September 16th, 2015 (Wide) (Indonesia)
Video Release: December 22nd, 2015 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense peril and disturbing images.
(Rating bulletin 2364, 3/11/2015)
Running Time: 121 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Digital Cinematography
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Disaster, Mountain Climbing, Extreme Weather
Social setting: Extreme Sports
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Working Title Films, Cross Creek Pictures, Walden Media, Universal Pictures, Free State Pictures, RVK
Production Countries: United States, United Kingdom
Languages: English

Synopsis

Inspired by the events surrounding an attempt to reach the summit of the world’s highest mountain, Everest documents the awe-inspiring journey of two different expeditions challenged beyond their limits by one of the fiercest snowstorms ever encountered by mankind. Their mettle tested by the harshest elements found on the planet, the climbers will face nearly impossible obstacles as a lifelong obsession becomes a breathtaking struggle for survival.
